name: Claire McCaskill
subject: scary dark money update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McCaskill for Missouri
body:
Friend -- the Koch network is only spending money on _four_ Senate races this year, and here's the scary news: We made the list. (Lucky us.)
**The Kochs are coming out against me because I'm leading the charge to get their dark money out of our politics.** And they're not alone. Right-wing dark money is pouring into Missouri -- and every outlet from Roll Call to The Washington Post reports that Missouri's Senate seat is one of the most vulnerable in the country.
The GOP is doing whatever it takes to flip this seat red, and I can't fight back without your help. But right now, my team's falling short of our big $10,000 goal ahead of the midnight deadline.
**[Please, rush every dollar you can to protect Missouri's only Democratic Senate seat and win in November.](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/mccaskill_email_fr?refcode=jtk1217-fr-nd-v2&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500)**

body:
See this? It's a gun. A very real one capable of firing this a number of times without malfunctioning. And it can be printed with commercially available printers. This one costs $399. Donald Trump's State Department recently allowed one company, Defense Distributed, to publish downloadable gun blueprints online for anyone to use. A court has temporarily blocked the publication of these blueprints, but the debate rages on.

So we're running a campaign demanding Congress pass legislation that would make that illegal. name: Tim Kaine
subject: Charlottesville, one year later
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | Derek -

One year ago today, a dangerous group of neo-Nazis and white supremacists brought hatred into Charlottesville.

**It was a catastrophe of significant proportions.** It was painful. It was ugly. And it led to the deaths of three Virginians: Heather Heyer, a paralegal who was peacefully demonstrating that Charlottesville was a place of love, not hate; and Lieutenant H. Jay Cullen and Trooper Berke M.M. Bates who were in a helicopter crash while surveilling the dangerous situation on the ground.

Some tried to pretend that this "Unite the Right" rally was about statues. **It wasn 't. It was about hatred.**

But over the past year, I've seen folks all across the Commonwealth - and, in fact, all across our nation - work together to stand up and say, "**no hate in our state**."

Right now, both our Commonwealth and America as a whole are facing real questions about whether we still believe in the last two words at the end of the Pledge of Allegiance. **Are we for _all_ , or not?**

So we are coming together and standing up to say, "**Yes, we are for all.** " People stood up for all when they turned out by the millions at Women's March and March for Our Lives events across the country. Folks stood up for all right here in Virginia, by turning out to the ballot box and electing new, forward-thinking leaders during last year's elections.

I meet people every day on the trail who are so energized and ready to stand up for all this year, because folks in this Commonwealth are more committed than ever to our state motto: **Virginia is for lovers. Not haters.**

So today, as we acknowledge the one-year anniversary of this very tragic day in our state's history, I just want to say to all Virginians, everyone who loves Virginians, and everyone who cares about Virginia and keeping our communities safe from violence: **This Commonwealth will continue to stand against hate.**

Thank you for reading,

_Tim_

subject: “us” vs. “them”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BREDESEN FOR SENATE
body:
#
Peter --
When I ran for re-election in 2006, **our campaign won all 95 counties here in Tennessee** \-- a first in our state’s modern history. We did it by bringing people together around common-sense solutions to the issues hurting our communities.
**If we’re going to win again, that kind of thoughtful approach -- rejecting the hyperpartisan climate in Washington -- is how we’ll do it.**
This race is not about “us” vs. “them.” It’s not about my opponent or me. It’s about Tennesseans -- our seniors, our students, and our families.
Tennesseans deserve better than a dysfunctional government full of politicians just shouting across the room at each other while our communities are suffering. That’s why this campaign is focused on fixing the mess in Washington and getting things done.
**But it’s going to take all of us stepping up and taking action to get this message in front of voters.**
[**That’s why I’m reaching out personally to ask if you’ll pitch in $10 or whatever you can to support our campaign. clean_body: Peter -- When I ran for re-election in 2006, our campaign won all 95 counties here in Tennessee \-- a first in our state’s modern history. We did it by bringing people together around common-sense solutions to the issues hurting our communities. If we’re going to win again, that kind of thoughtful approach -- rejecting the hyperpartisan climate in Washington -- is how we’ll do it. This race is not about “us” vs. “them.” It’s not about my opponent or me. It’s about Tennesseans -- our seniors, our students, and our families. Tennesseans deserve better than a dysfunctional government full of politicians just shouting across the room at each other while our communities are suffering. That’s why this campaign is focused on fixing the mess in Washington and getting things done. But it’s going to take all of us stepping up and taking action to get this message in front of voters. If we can win this race the right way -- bringing people together instead of dividing them -- then we’ll have a real shot at breaking through the partisan fighting and gridlock in Congress. Thanks for all you do, —Phil Bredesen PAID FOR BY BREDESEN FOR SENATE P.O. Box 92010 Nashville, TN 37209

Last weekend, Libertarians nominated Gary Johnson to challenge me in my re-election campaign, and this could change everything in my race.

Johnson, a former two-term governor of New Mexico and presidential candidate, has a national network of wealthy donors to bankroll his campaign. They already have a super PAC set up, ready to flood the airwaves with ads against me the moment Johnson gets in the race.

subject: Have you voted yet?
body:
** The primary election day is this Tuesday!
------------------------------------------------------------

Early voting has been going on for some time now, and we are finally nearing election day. Even though Ian does not have a primary challenger it is still important to get out to support the other DFL candidates. It is also an important day because AJ Kern has been aggressively challenging Tom Emmer on the Republican side, and we will find out who Ian will face in November. You can read more about AJ below. Don't forget to get out and vote for all of your favorite DFLers this Tuesday, and if you have already voted then thank you!

** We have been out and about getting people motivated!
------------------------------------------------------------
Our team has been out making phone calls, knocking on doors and showing up to parades. We want to make sure no matter who we are facing in the general election that we are ready to win!
The Republican primary in this district looks like it will be much closer than it was last time, which means we don't know who we will be facing in November. Donate today (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/iantodd?refcode=preprimary&utm_source=Newsletter&utm_campaign=afd3e9b7a6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_08_12_03_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_28710cd7a6-afd3e9b7a6-9291445) to help our campaign be prepared no matter the outcome this Tuesday.
Support our Campaign (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/iantodd?refcode=preprimary&utm_source=Newsletter&utm_campaign=afd3e9b7a6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_08_12_03_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_28710cd7a6-afd3e9b7a6-9291445)

** What is happening on the Republican side?
------------------------------------------------------------
Surprisingly enough AJ Kern has been aggressively primarying Tom Emmer from the right and has spent most of her time and effort fearmongering on the issues of immigration and refugees. You can read more about her fearmongering here (http://www.citypages.com/news/st-cloud-times-columnist-aj-kern-fans-the-flames-of-anti-refugee-fears-7974107?utm_source=Newsletter&utm_campaign=afd3e9b7a6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_08_12_03_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_28710cd7a6-afd3e9b7a6-9291445) . She won 26% of the vote last cycle and stands as a formidable opponent for Tom this year.

If she wins we need to be able to stand strong and get our message out that Minnesotans will not tolerate this type of fear in our politics. We need your help to be prepared to get our message out donate today (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/iantodd?refcode=preprimary&utm_source=Newsletter&utm_campaign=afd3e9b7a6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_08_12_03_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_28710cd7a6-afd3e9b7a6-9291445) to make sure that we can stand against fearmongering in our district.

name: J.D. Scholten
subject: What a difference a year makes.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Peter,
Friday night was special milestone for me. I returned to speak in front of 1,000 people at Iowa’s Annual Wing Ding, the place where one year ago today, I gave my first big speech.

Last year, most people at the event didn’t even know who I was. On Friday, I couldn’t walk 10 feet without somebody stopping me to take a selfie or wish me luck.
Last year I had two brand new staff I wasn’t sure I could pay, now I’ve got 10 of the best dang team members I’ve ever known.

What a difference a year makes.
A year ago I stood there and dared everyone to tell me this was an unwinnable race. Because for too long I had been told Steve King was unbeatable.

Today, look around this country:
Conor Lamb is in congress representing what they once called “Trump Country.” Doug Jones is a Democratic Senator in Alabama. Just days ago in Ohio, Danny O’Connor ran in a special election for a seat in Congress the Republicans have held since 1983 and came within inches of victory – hungering for the November rematch. **And in our primary this year almost 10,000 more people voted for someone not named Steve King, than voted for him.**

People are waking up and calling for real leadership, including here in Iowa. **They are using their right to vote to vote for what is right.**

Let me tell you a story. A few months back I was doing a meet-and-greet, when this guy comes in, hot under the collar, and forces his way into the conversation. “What do you know about farmers?!” he asked. “How are you going to beat King?!” I guess it made some of the people uncomfortable. We went toe-to-toe on the issues for a few minutes. Afterward, my team got a call from one of those folks, apologizing, saying he is the county bully and a well-known King guy. Well, if that’s bullying, I really hope every single one of you is just as tough. **Because the only things that guy left me with were a check for $50 and a probable vote.**
I’ve been seeing it for over a year now – there is a hunger in this district and an energy that matches what we’re seeing around the country.

For the last year, I have been trying show what a Democrat from Iowa’s 4th looks like. Here are some of the figures:
* It’s 35,000 miles driven across the 39 counties of this district in my own car.
* It’s another 10,000 miles doing the same at the wheel of my campaign’s Winnebago, “Sioux City Sue,” built right here in the district. It’s almost a million dollars raised from over 12,000 individuals with an average campaign donation of $18.44 and without taking ANY corporate PAC money.
* It’s at least 200 slices of Casey’s pizza, and 1,000 cups of their coffee.
* It’s marching in countless town parades and speaking at dozens of town meetings in counties that haven’t seen Steve King hold any public events for years.
* It’s the support of Iowans. It’s the support of **you.**

We are what an Iowa Democrat looks like.

At heart I am a town kid one generation away from the farm. And when I am elected I will work to help the people of this district, whatever party label they want to attach to themselves. Because health care, education, preserving the farm, opportunity—a government and an economy that works for us—these are not partisan issues.

In case you haven’t been following my race: my campaign didn’t just come to wear a uniform. We came to compete. **We came to win. **
**To Steve King, I have one message for you... I look forward to the debates.**
Standing Tall for All,
J.D. Scholten
_**[P.S. Despite all that we’ve accomplished in this last year, we’re only getting started. Help us keep it going with a contribution today!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1888943/9915080/1387774481?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNGVlZDAzNC03MjllLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YeOyA6V92u6LxoNBaoJ1p1Bf4LRqTioDLJGaMX_W1O4=)**_

name: Bob Menendez
subject: One of America's darkest hours
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Menendez for Senate
body:
Friend,

I never imagined that in the year 2017 we would see our country step backward on issues of justice and equality. But one year ago today in Charlottesville, Virginia, white nationalists were emboldened to take to the streets and spew hatred.

That same bigotry led to the murder of Heather Heyer. Heather's tragic death was at the hands of a white nationalist as she fought for justice and equality for everyone at the white supremacist rally in Charlottesville - the very rally where President Trump suggested there were "very fine folks on both sides."

The extreme hate we saw that day was no doubt one of America's darkest hours in recent memory. But that darkness was followed with an outpouring of brave and courageous young people fighting back and standing up for decency. I urge you to join in and fight back against bigotry and hatred whenever we see it.

As Americans, we cannot accept this type of action in our streets. Our nation has come a long way, but there is so much more work to be done. Join me today in honoring the sacrifice of brave heroes like Heather.

Justice and equality for everyone.

Bob

clean_body: Friend, I never imagined that in the year 2017 we would see our country step backward on issues of justice and equality. But one year ago today in Charlottesville, Virginia, white nationalists were emboldened to take to the streets and spew hatred. That same bigotry led to the murder of Heather Heyer. Heather's tragic death was at the hands of a white nationalist as she fought for justice and equality for everyone at the white supremacist rally in Charlottesville - the very rally where President Trump suggested there were "very fine folks on both sides." The extreme hate we saw that day was no doubt one of America's darkest hours in recent memory. But that darkness was followed with an outpouring of brave and courageous young people fighting back and standing up for decency. I urge you to join in and fight back against bigotry and hatred whenever we see it. As Americans, we cannot accept this type of action in our streets. Our nation has come a long way, but there is so much more work to be done. Join me today in honoring the sacrifice of brave heroes like Heather. Justice and equality for everyone. Bob | Paid for by Menendez for Senate Contributions or gifts to Menendez for Senate are not tax deductible. "" ""

body:
Hello,
Minnesota's Primary Election is in just two days, and it's an important moment to step back and take stock.
What's at stake in this election? Nothing less than all the progress we've made together -- like protecting health care for Americans with pre-existing medical conditions -- and all the progress we still can, and must, make together.

We need to stay active, stay engaged, and win big victories this year. We have an important deadline coming up -- our FEC Pre-Primary Filing Deadline on August 16th. "" If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: "" "" "" "" I will not be silent while Republicans sit in their committee chairs and make decisions for women in doctors' waiting room chairs all across this country. We can't slack off this year. We can't sit on the sidelines and hope that someone else speaks out, someone else gives money, and someone else turns out to vote. We all need to stand up, speak out, and work hard. "" | | | | "" Rest assured, I will fight to protect a woman's right to choose. Thank you for fighting beside me. Debbie | | | Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress

name: Cory Booker
subject: Be a difference maker in Georgia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
Stacey is fighting for progress in Georgia, but she needs our help. | | | |
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa95b/34a064cc/ae1115d/70e8273/1643815617/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwODEyMTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-booker?refcode=IE081218-full")

Friends - I can't say this enough: Stacey Abrams inspires me, and I'm doing everything I can to help her win in November. But Stacey needs our support now more than ever. In late July, Brian Kemp became her Republican opponent, and Kemp, Trump, and the GOP establishment are already attacking her. **[Will you help Stacey Abrams fight back by making a donation to her campaign today?](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa95b/34a064cc/ae1115d/70e8273/1643815617/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwODEyMTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-booker?refcode=IE081218-full")** This race is critical for Georgia's future. From health care, child care, and college affordability, to criminal justice reform and economic opportunity for working families, Stacey's vision for her state will transform our nation. But democracy's not a spectator sport, and we've got to get in the game. Just last month, I was in Georgia helping Stacey reach out to voters - because this race is that important. Stacey will need all hands on deck, and I encourage you to join her campaign as a volunteer. Whether or not you can spare that time - and I truly hope you can - I encourage you to say loud and clear that "I'm in" by making a contribution today. If you believe in Stacey Abrams as much as I do - and you want to help elect bold, progressive leaders in every state - stand with me: **[Chip in $3 to help Stacey Abrams fight for Georgia families as governor.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa95b/34a064cc/ae1115d/70e8273/1643815617/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwODEyMTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-booker?refcode=IE081218-full")** Defeating Republican Brian Kemp in a state like Georgia won't be easy, but if we all step up to do what we can, we will push Stacey to victory. So whether you're able to volunteer or donate to support voter contact, be a difference maker in Georgia. After all, history is never made by one individual - it's made by the people who believed, who worked, who contributed, and who served to make progress possible. Thanks, Cory

**[DONATE](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa95b/34a064cc/ae1115d/70e8273/1643815617/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwODEyMTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-booker?refcode=IE081218-full")**

_Senator Booker is asking for contributions of up to $2,700 per election from individuals and $5,000 per election from multicandidate PACs. He is not asking for contributions in excess of the federal limits, or from corporations, labor organizations, and/or other federally prohibited sources._

Friend,

Last Tuesday, I wrote to you when we were EXACTLY one week away from election day and we set a big goal.

$100,000 before election day to keep us going strong.

Thanks to great supporters like you, I’m happy to say we’ve been able to keep up our momentum in the field, on TV, radio, and digital... but after today, that’s going to change. We’re not going to have enough to keep us going these last few days.

I know so many of you have already stepped up, but we still need all hands on deck to make this work.

If we want to continue our get out the vote efforts over the weekend, we really need $10,000 by midnight. TONIGHT.

clean_body: Last Tuesday, I wrote to you when we were EXACTLY one week away from election day and we set a big goal. Friend, Last Tuesday, I wrote to you when we were EXACTLY one week away from election day and we set a big goal. $100,000 before election day to keep us going strong. Thanks to great supporters like you, I’m happy to say we’ve been able to keep up our momentum in the field, on TV, radio, and digital... but after today, that’s going to change. We’re not going to have enough to keep us going these last few days. I know so many of you have already stepped up, but we still need all hands on deck to make this work. As you know, we’re really down to the wire on this race... we cannot afford to come up short. Please, friend. Chip in now before it’s too late. Thanks, Leah \----------- forwarded message below ----------- Before we head into the weekend, I have to ask you for a big favor, Friend. We’re just four days out from Tuesday’s primary and we’re still working hard to reach our $100K goal so we can make sure we're victorious. If we want to continue our get out the vote efforts over the weekend, we really need $10,000 by midnight. TONIGHT. Friend, that will help pay for transportation, food, and water for our hardworking volunteers who will be getting out the vote this weekend. Donating even $1 would help us buy a bottle of water for our team. $5 would mean we could grab a lunch for someone who has been knocking on doors or making calls all day. $100 could mean snacks for one local office. ANYTHING helps. We just need to make sure we reach this goal by midnight so our field teams can plan for the weekend. Please, chip in now. Thanks, Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate
